title: Add --run to artifacts and harden 404 errors
description: Walkthrough of three file changes: --run flag support in artifacts command, router forwarding in index, and 404 error handling in artifact command.

steps.title: Import getFlag utility
steps.description: Added import for getFlag from ../lib/flags.js to enable CLI flag parsing in the artifacts command.

steps.title: Add args parameter to function signature
steps.description: Changed function signature from (config, ticketId) to (config, ticketId, args) to accept CLI arguments, mirroring the pattern in artifact.ts and bundle.ts.

steps.title: Read --run flag
steps.description: Extract the optional --run flag value from CLI args using getFlag. Returns string or undefined.

steps.title: Conditional queryParams forwarding
steps.description: When runId is defined, include it as a queryParams entry on the hxFetch call using object spread. When undefined, no queryParams are sent, preserving existing default behavior.

steps.title: Usage text updated
steps.description: Updated the artifacts subcommand usage text to show [--run <runId>] so users can discover the new option.

steps.title: Forward rest args to cmdTicketsArtifacts
steps.description: Changed router call from cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, ticketId) to cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, ticketId, rest), matching the pattern already used for artifact (line 85) and bundle (line 91).

steps.title: Declare data variable before try block
steps.description: Moved the StepArtifactResponse variable declaration outside the try block so it remains accessible after the try-catch.

steps.title: Try-catch wraps hxFetch call
steps.description: Wrapped the hxFetch call in a try-catch to intercept HTTP errors (including 404 for missing artifacts) before they propagate to the global error handler.

steps.title: Clean error message with context
steps.description: On catch, prints a user-friendly error message including the step and repo names for context, plus the original error message for debugging. Then exits with code 1.
